California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 18, 1990
willian H. Randolph
chief Administrative off icer
county of Butte
25 county Center Drive
orovil-1,e, CA 95965-3380
Re:
Dear Mr. Randolph:
This is i.n response to your November 5, ]-990, letter
requesting advice concerning the conflict of interest provisions
of the Political 
Reform Act ("Act't).r 
Your request is one for
general assistance. Accordingly, vJe are treating it as a request
for informal assistance.z/
You have asked the Fair Political Practices Cornmission (the
rr Cornmiss ionrt ) to review Butte county's proposed amendments to
their conflict of interest code prior to the public hearing-
A conflict of interest code is a document which lists the
positions within the agency that are in deci.sion rnaking capacities
lnd further provides disclosure categories which describe the
types of information to be reported by the employees whose
poiitions are designated in the code. The categories are designed
to require a designated emptoyee's dj.sclosure of the economic
interests whj-ch can be affected by decisions the designated
employee rnakes in his or her official. capacity.
You would not designate in the code your county board of
supervisors (board supervisors are already covered under Section
1 co,r"r.,."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. Al-l- statutory references
are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
Comrnission
regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations Section
18ooo, et ses. AI1 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Reqnrlations.
2/ 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
irnmunity provided by an opinion or formaL written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114,' 2 Ca1. Code of Regs. Section
1832e (c) (3). )
Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. f-9 0-6 9 4
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 95804-0807 . (916) 322.5660


Our File No. I-9 0-59 4
Page 2
8720o and have county-wide jurisdiction), 
nor would you designate
other county employees uho are required to file under Section
8720o and have been statutoril-y assigned fu]I disclosure.
Section 87302 requires that a conflict of interest code
enumerate the positions designated, other than those sDecified in
section 87200, which involve naking or participating in naking
decisions which may foreseeably have a ruaterial effect on a
financial interest. 
Each such desigmated employee is required to
file staternents at specified tines and disclose certain interests.
Pursuant to Section 8201L. the code reviewing body for a
single county agency is its board of supervisors. A11 single city
agencies report to their city council. 
A11 nuLti-county
jurisdictional 
agencies fa11 under the authority of the
comrniss ion.
The county of Butte is proposing an amendment to its conflict
of interest code. You have written to the commission for
assistance and any advice or suggestions as to the accuracy of
your proposed amendrnents. As the Commission is not your agency's
Loae ieviewing body, we are unable to assist you specifically in
the preparation of your conflict of interest code, nor can $re
pror-id"- a detaiLed revj.ew of your list of designated positions and
whether they have been assigned appropriate disclosure
requirements. However, lre are able to provide general assistance
in relation to areas of your code which may require correction and
provide suggestions to their solutions.
we have had an opportunity to review your county's proposed
amendments to its conilict of interest code and have the following
suggestions:
1) 
Based upon a cursory review of your appendix of
disclosure categories, ere have the following conments and
suggestions :
within each disclosure category you should remove
any language referring to applicable filing schedules. Removing
this language !ri1] prevent you from having to make unnecessary and
tine consuming code anendments in the future. 
If you \.rere to keep
this language in your code, you would be requi.red to arnend your
code each and every time that a statutory change were nade
affecting the numbering sequence of the filing forms, i.e.,
Schedules A, B, C, ...etc.
It is commendable that you are providing descriptive filing
assistance to your designated positions. 
Perhaps, an alternative
$rouLd be to distribute a cover nemo or factsheet to your
employees, listing the applicable filing 
schedules to their
spLciiic dj.sclosure categories. rn this way, you would continue
to provide the assitance to your designees, yet in such a way as
not to cause unnecessary amendments to your conflict of interest
code.


Our Fi■e No. I-90-694
Page 3
Dj.sclosure category vI , subParagraph 3, should be amended to
include the reporting of rrincome from sourcesrr. category VI ,
subparagraph 3 should read:
Investments and business positions in business entities or
nonprofit corporations and income fron sources which are of
the type which provide consul.tant services to any business
entity or nonprofit corporation nade reportable by this
disclosure category.
2) 
You have correctly designated the position of Chief
Investment officer in your conflict of interest code. At this
point in tirne, we are instructinq agencies to continue including
iilers 
who manage public investments in their conflict of j.nterest
codes until such time as the Cornmission has clarified the scope of
application of the term t'other public officials 
who manaqe public
investrnents.rr (Section 872OO, factsheet enclosed.)
I hope that the information contained in this letter is of
some help to you. If I can be of any further assj-stance, please
do not hesitate to contact ne at (916) 322-590]-.
S incere 1y ,
scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

Dear Mr. Rynearson:
You have requested advice concerning the carnpaign provisions

FACTS
A group of individuals has provided funds for an ad in sup-
port of Anna Sparks for Supervisor. The group is not controlled
by a candidate. Individual payments were made to a newspaper by
each individual for the purchase of an ad on November 2, 1990.
The total amount paid for the ad exceeded g1,o0o. The group has
filed a statement of organization (Form 4l-O) as the Friends of
Sparks and a statement of termination (Form 415) and has providecl
the filing officer vith a copy of the checks used to make the
expenditure and a list of all contributors.
OI]ESTION
Is the group a rtcommittee'r with filing obligations under the
Act and, if so, has the cornrnittee followed the correct procedure
for reporting receipts and expenditures?

sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses two separate matters: (1) whether Butte County's proposed amendments to its conflict of interest code are accurate and what general assistance can be provided, and (2) whether a group funding an ad for a supervisor candidate constitutes a 'committee' with filing obligations and if correct procedures were followed.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: For the conflict of interest code matter: The Commission cannot provide detailed review as it is not the code reviewing body, but provides general suggestions including removing filing schedule references from the code and amending disclosure category VI to include income from sources. For the campaign committee matter: The group appears to have formed a committee with filing obligations, and late contribution reports may be filed by certified mail rather than telegram if discovered after an election.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; Skipped (too short): conclusion; CONCLUSION has only 0 words; LLM: Document contains two separate advice letters merged together. First letter (I-90-694) is informal advice with minimal conclusion section. Second letter (A-90-695) has garbled/OCR-error text in the conclusion and analysis sections making full extraction difficult. Both letters signed by Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The document addresses two separate matters: (1) whether Butte County's proposed amendments to its conflict of interest code are accurate and what general assistance can be provided, and (2) whether a group funding an ad for a supervisor candidate constitutes a 'committee' with filing obligations and if correct procedures were followed.

CONCLUSION: For the conflict of interest code matter: The Commission cannot provide detailed review as it is not the code reviewing body, but provides general suggestions including removing filing schedule references from the code and amending disclosure category VI to include income from sources. For the campaign committee matter: The group appears to have formed a committee with filing obligations, and late contribution reports may be filed by certified mail rather than telegram if discovered after an election.

embedding.summary: This document contains two FPPC advice letters: one providing informal assistance to Butte County regarding amendments to its conflict of interest code (Document I-90-694), and another regarding whether a group funding campaign advertisements constitutes a committee with filing obligations under the Political Reform Act (Document A-90-695).
